Tool_nifty_50_returns_Description: Project NIFTY 50-style returns with lumpsum or SIP and an assumed CAGR. Compare 8%, 12%, and 15% scenarios.
Tool_nifty_50_returns_LearnText: Enter lumpsum or monthly SIP, years, and an assumed CAGR (default 12%). See invested amount, estimated value, and gain — plus fixed scenarios at 8%, 12%, and 15%. Not historical backtesting.
Tool_nifty_50_returns_FormulaNote: Assumed CAGR is illustrative. Past NIFTY returns do not guarantee future results.
Tool_nifty_50_returns_CagrHint: Default 12% is a common long-term equity assumption — adjust as you like.
Tool_nifty_50_returns_ExampleStep_5: Compare the same SIP at 8% and 15% in the scenario cards.
Tool_nifty_50_returns_Step_1: Choose lumpsum or SIP and enter amount.
Tool_nifty_50_returns_Step_3: Compute FV with compound / SIP formula.
Tool_nifty_50_returns_Info_Purpose: Estimate future value of a NIFTY-style equity investment under an assumed CAGR.
Tool_nifty_50_returns_Info_WhyKnow: Index returns vary year to year; scenario ranges help set expectations.
Tool_nifty_50_returns_Info_RealLife: Planning SIPs into index funds or ETFs tracking NIFTY 50.
Tool_nifty_50_returns_Info_Goal_1: See FV under your assumed CAGR
Tool_nifty_50_returns_Info_Alt_Sip_When: For generic SIP math without NIFTY framing.
Tool_nifty_50_returns_Info_Alt_Cagr_When: When you already know start and end values.
Tool_nifty_50_returns_Info_Differentiati: FinYatra shows lumpsum/SIP FV with fixed 8/12/15% NIFTY-style scenarios in one view.
Tool_nifty_50_returns_Info_Motto: Assume a CAGR — then stress-test it.
Tool_real_estate_Description: Estimate equity build-up, EMI, and rent income over your hold period — simplified property ROI.
Tool_real_estate_LearnText: Enter property price, down payment %, loan rate and tenure, expected appreciation, and rent yield. See EMI, future property value, equity after loan paydown, and cumulative rent over the hold period.
Tool_real_estate_FormulaNote: Simplified: no taxes, maintenance, vacancy, or stamp duty. Educational only.
Tool_real_estate_YieldHint: Annual rent as % of purchase price (before expenses).
Tool_real_estate_ScenarioSubtitle: Equity at lower / base / higher appreciation
Tool_real_estate_ExampleStep_1: Down = 20% → loan = ₹64 lakh; EMI from reducing-balance formula.
Tool_real_estate_ExampleStep_2: After 10 years, property FV at 5% appreciation.
Tool_real_estate_ExampleStep_5: Illustrative net = equity + rent − down payment.
Tool_real_estate_Step_1: Enter price, down %, loan rate and tenure.
Tool_real_estate_Step_2: Set appreciation, rent yield, and hold years.
Tool_real_estate_Step_3: Compute EMI, FV, and loan balance after hold.
Tool_real_estate_Step_4: Read equity, rent, and illustrative net.
Tool_real_estate_Info_Purpose: Roughly project property equity and rental income over a hold period.
Tool_real_estate_Info_WhyKnow: Purchase price alone hides leverage, EMI, and rent cash flows.
Tool_real_estate_Info_RealLife: Comparing buy-and-hold vs renting, or sizing a home loan.
Tool_real_estate_Info_Goal_3: Know monthly EMI on the loan
Tool_real_estate_Info_Alt_Home_When: When you only need EMI and interest.
Tool_real_estate_Info_Alt_Rent_When: For landlord tax or tenant proofs.
Tool_real_estate_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra combines EMI, appreciation equity, and rent yield in one simplified hold-period view.
Tool_real_estate_Info_Motto: Equity is price growth minus what you still owe.
Tool_lic_premium_Description: Rough annual premium estimate from sum assured, age, term, and rate per thousand — educational, not a quote.
Tool_lic_premium_LearnText: Enter sum assured, age, policy term, and a rough premium rate per ₹1,000 SA. The tool applies a small illustrative age loading and shows annual premium, total premiums over the term, and premium-to-cover ratio.
Tool_lic_premium_FormulaNote: Not an LIC quote. Real premiums depend on plan, health, riders, and underwriting.
Tool_lic_premium_RateHint: Example: ₹8 means ₹8 per year for every ₹1,000 of cover (before age loading).
Tool_lic_premium_ScenarioSubtitle: Same cover at lower / base / higher rate per thousand
Tool_lic_premium_ExampleStep_2: Age 30 → no age loading on base rate 8.
Tool_lic_premium_ExampleStep_5: Compare with ±2 rate scenarios for a range.
Tool_lic_premium_Step_1: Enter sum assured, age, and term.
Tool_lic_premium_Step_3: Apply illustrative age loading if age > 30.
Tool_lic_premium_Step_4: Read annual and total premium estimates.
Tool_lic_premium_Info_Purpose: Ballpark annual premium from sum assured and a rate-per-thousand assumption.
Tool_lic_premium_Info_WhyKnow: Quotes vary widely; a rough model helps size cover vs budget before talking to an agent.
Tool_lic_premium_Info_RealLife: Term vs endowment planning and 80C budgeting (plan-dependent).
Tool_lic_premium_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra shows a transparent rate-per-thousand estimate with age loading — clearly labelled as not a quote.
Tool_lic_premium_Info_Motto: Size the cover first — then get a real quote.
Tool_nps_vs_ops_Description: Compare NPS corpus and annuity pension with a simplified OPS formula (50% of last pay) for government employees.
Tool_nps_vs_ops_LearnText: Project your NPS corpus from monthly contributions and returns, estimate annuity pension (40% corpus), and compare with simplified OPS: 50% of projected last pay. Educational — not UPS or official pension rules.
Tool_nps_vs_ops_FormulaNote: OPS here is a simplified 50% of last pay. Actual OPS/UPS rules, commutation, and family pension differ.
Tool_nps_vs_ops_PayHint: Used as starting pay; grown to retirement for OPS.
Tool_nps_vs_ops_Result_GapFootnoteNps: NPS pension higher in this model
Tool_nps_vs_ops_Result_GapFootnoteOps: OPS pension higher in this model
Tool_nps_vs_ops_ScenarioSubtitle: NPS pension at lower / base / higher returns
Tool_nps_vs_ops_ExampleStep_1: Years = 30; grow pay at 5% for OPS last-pay estimate.
Tool_nps_vs_ops_ExampleStep_3: NPS corpus = FV(existing) + SIP FV of contributions.
Tool_nps_vs_ops_ExampleStep_4: Annuity corpus = 40% of NPS; monthly ≈ (annuity × rate) / 12.
Tool_nps_vs_ops_Step_1: Enter age, retirement, pay, and NPS inputs.
Tool_nps_vs_ops_Step_3: Grow NPS corpus; take 40% for annuity.
Tool_nps_vs_ops_Info_Abbreviation: NPS vs OPS — govt pension compare
Tool_nps_vs_ops_Info_Purpose: Illustrate NPS market-linked pension vs a simplified OPS last-pay formula.
Tool_nps_vs_ops_Info_WhyKnow: Govt employees often need a clear side-by-side before retirement planning.
Tool_nps_vs_ops_Info_RealLife: Central/state employees under NPS comparing with old pension expectations.
Tool_nps_vs_ops_Info_Alt_Annuity_When: To model annuity payouts from a corpus.
Tool_nps_vs_ops_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra puts simplified OPS (50% last pay) next to NPS corpus and annuity pension in one comparison.
Tool_nps_vs_ops_Info_Motto: Compare the monthly cheque — not just the corpus.
Tool_marriage_budget_Description: Add major wedding expense buckets and a contingency % to see your total marriage budget.
Tool_marriage_budget_LearnText: Enter venue, catering, décor, attire, photography, jewellery, travel, and miscellaneous costs. Add a contingency percent for surprises — see subtotal, buffer, and grand total.
Tool_marriage_budget_FormulaNote: Planning aid only. Actual wedding costs vary widely by city and scale.
Tool_marriage_budget_ScenarioSubtitle: Same expenses at 5%, your %, and 15% buffer
Tool_marriage_budget_ExampleStep_1: Add venue, catering, décor, attire, photo, jewellery, travel, misc.
Tool_marriage_budget_Info_Purpose: Total major wedding costs with a contingency buffer.
Tool_marriage_budget_Info_WhyKnow: Weddings overrun easily without a clear bucketed budget.
Tool_marriage_budget_Info_RealLife: Families planning savings and guest-scale trade-offs.
Tool_marriage_budget_Info_Alt_Goal_When: To save toward the wedding total.
Tool_marriage_budget_Info_Alt_Sip_When: For investment growth toward the event.
Tool_marriage_budget_Info_Differentiatio: FinYatra lists wedding expense buckets and contingency in one clear total.
Tool_marriage_budget_Info_Motto: Budget the celebration — then enjoy it.
Tool_electric_vehicle_Description: Compare ICE vs EV running costs per km and estimate payback on the EV purchase price premium.
Tool_electric_vehicle_LearnText: Enter fuel cost per km (ICE), electricity cost per km (EV), monthly kilometres, and years. Optionally add how much more the EV costs to buy — see running savings and payback years.
Tool_electric_vehicle_FormulaNote: Ignores maintenance, battery degradation, subsidies, and resale. Illustrative TCO slice only.
Tool_electric_vehicle_DeltaHint: How much more the EV costs vs ICE. Set 0 to skip payback.
Tool_electric_vehicle_ExampleSubtitle: ₹6/km ICE vs ₹1.5/km EV, ₹2 lakh premium, 5 years
Tool_electric_vehicle_Info_Purpose: Compare petrol/diesel vs electric running costs and estimate premium payback.
Tool_electric_vehicle_Info_WhyKnow: Lower ₹/km only pays off if you drive enough to recover the higher sticker price.
Tool_electric_vehicle_Info_RealLife: Choosing between a petrol hatchback and an EV for city/highway mix.
Tool_electric_vehicle_Info_Goal_1: Compare ICE vs EV running cost
Tool_electric_vehicle_Info_Alt_Budget_Wh: To fit fuel/charging into monthly spend.
Tool_electric_vehicle_Info_Differentiati: FinYatra shows ICE vs EV ₹/km totals and optional purchase-premium payback side by side.
Tool_electric_vehicle_Info_Motto: Cheap per km only wins if you drive the kilometres.
